data.title: Summit Hotel Properties announces acquisition of two-hotel portfolio for $96M
data.description:
• Summit Hotel Properties (NYSE:INN), through its joint venture with GIC, announced on Tuesday that it is under contract to acquire the 250-guestroom Hampton Inn Boston - Logan Airport and the 149-guestroom Hilton Garden Inn Tysons Corner for a combined purchase price of $96M, or $241,000 per key.
  • The purchase price represents an 8.8% capitalization rate based on forecasted 2024 net operating income and no meaningful capital expenditures are planned before the fourth quarter of 2026.
  • The transaction is expected to be financed with a mix of cash on hand and a $50 million term loan raised with exercising a portion of the accordion option on the GIC joint venture's existing $200 million credit facility.
  • The term loan will have an interest rate of SOFR + 210 basis points, and a fully extended maturity date of September 2028.
  • The transaction is expected to close in the fourth quarter of 2024.
  • Summit Hotel Properties (INN) shares were up 1.16% in post-market trading.

data.title: Chevron completes upgrade at Pasadena refinery to boost capacity, product flexibility
data.description:
Chevron (NYSE:CVX) said Tuesday it completed an expansion of its Pasadena, Texas, refinery that will allow it to process more light crude oil from the Permian Basin and supply more products to customers on the U.S. Gulf Coast.

The company said the Light Tight Oil project should increase the facility's capacity to process lighter grades of crude oil by nearly 15% to 125K bbl/day, and increase the refinery's product flexibility, allowing it to produce jet fuel and export gas oil.

Chevron (CVX) also expects to realize cost savings by having the Pasadena facility work in tandem with its 375K bbl/day Pascagoula refinery in Mississippi.

Some of the additional gasoline produced at Pasadena may be earmarked for new Chevron (CVX) retail stations in Texas or Louisiana, and the facility also may produce some feedstock for the complex Pascagoula refinery, OPIS reports.

Chevron (CVX) last year produced ~360K bbl/day of crude oil from its Permian Basin operations.

data.title: Dave & Buster's Entertainment loss widens, CEO resigns
data.description:
Dave & Buster’s Entertainment (NASDAQ:PLAY) reported a wider-than-expected loss in the third quarter as the impact from a material fiscal calendar mismatch, adverse weather conditions, remodel construction, and certain “unusual” items during the quarter negatively impacted the company’s profitability.

Coupled with the announcement that CEO Chris Morris has resigned, shares fell as much as 15% in after-hours trading.

Following Morris's resignation, the board appointed current chairman Kevin Sheehan as interim CEO. The board has also appointed Director James Chambers as Vice Chair, and Michael Griffith as lead independent director.

For the reported quarter, Dave & Buster’s (PLAY) comparable store sales were down 7.7%, contributing to a 3% decline in revenue to $453M and a loss of $0.45 per share, both of which missed Wall Street’s expectations by $10.7M and 9 cents, respectively. Last year, the company lost a penny a share.

Adjusted EBITDA totaled $68.3M, or 15.1% of revenue, compared to $81.6M, or 17.5% of revenue in the same quarter last year.

By segment, entertainment sales fell by 2.6%, food and beverage sales were down 4.2%, while cost of entertainment and food and beverages decreased by 18% and 7%, respectively.

The company did not provide forward guidance.

data.title: Nutanix announces private offering of $750 million of convertible senior notes offering
data.description:
• Nutanix (NASDAQ:NTNX) on Tuesday announced its intention to offer $750M of convertible senior notes due 2029 in a private placement.
  • The company also expects to grant the initial purchasers of the notes an option to purchase up to an additional $112.5M of the notes within a 13-day period from, and including, the initial issuance date of the notes.
  • The notes will be unsecured senior obligations of Nutanix. Interest will be payable semi-annually in arrears. The notes will mature on December 15, 2029, unless earlier converted, redeemed, or repurchased.
  • The notes will be convertible into cash, shares of Nutanix’s class A common stock or a combination of cash and shares of Nutanix’s Class A common stock, at Nutanix’s election.
  • The interest rate, initial conversion rate and other terms of the notes are to be determined at the time of the pricing of the offering.
  • Nutanix intends to use the net proceeds to repurchase a portion of its outstanding 0.25% convertible senior notes due 2027 concurrently with the pricing of the offering.
  • The proceeds will be also used to repurchase up to $200.0 million of shares of Nutanix’s Class A common stock, the company said.
  • NTNX -0.73% after hours to $63.7.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: ONGold announces private placement
data.description:
• ONGold Resources (TSXV:ONAU:CA) intends to complete a non-brokered flow-through private placement offering for aggregate gross proceeds of up to $3M.
  • Under the offering, the company will issue up to 4.6M common shares of the company that will qualify as "flow-through shares" for the purposes of the Income Tax Act at a price of $0.65 per FT Share.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: BofA restarts coverage of 11 large-cap biopharmas
data.description:
BofA has reinstated coverage of 11 large-cap pharmaceutical and biotech stocks, with Eli Lilly (NYSE:LLY), Gilead (NASDAQ:GILD) and Merck (NYSE:MRK) receiving buy ratings.

BofA said Lilly (LLY) should be a core holding due to its “outsized, durable long-term growth.” The bank noted that Lilly (LLY) and obesity drug rival Novo Nordisk (NVO) both have “many protective moats around them" and set a price objective of $997 for the stock.

Gilead (GILD) was deemed “a relatively quiet stock” with “above-average durable long-term growth – at a below-average P/E multiple." BoA’s price objective for Gilead (GILD) is $109.

The investment bank called Merck (MRK) “arguably the best GARP name in the category,” adding that its P/E multiple was “too low for the long-term growth MRK offers.” The bank’s price objective for Merck (MRK) is $121.

Meanwhile, AbbVie (NYSE:ABBV), Biogen (NASDAQ:BIIB), Bristol Myers Squibb (NYSE:BMY), Johnson & Johnson (NYSE:JNJ) and Pfizer (NYSE:PFE) were all rated neutral. The bank set prices objectives of $191 for AbbVie (ABBV), $178 for Biogen (BIIB), $63 for Bristol Myers (BMY), $166 for J&J (JNJ) and $29 for Pfizer (PFE).

BofA called AbbVie (ABBV) another “quiet” stock with durable long-term growth and above-average valuation. The bank said Biogen (BIIB) appeared to have limited downside and was “worth tracking as Leqembi launch picks up.”

For Bristol Meyers (BMY), BofA said further upside would be difficult given the stock’s recent run-up. J&J (JNJ) was also seen with limited upside at current valuation, although a resolution to its talc litigation in 2025 “would help.”

The bank said that uncertainty over Pfizer’s (PFE) Covid revenue and base business outlook “explains the very low P/E, but keeps it there.” It added that the company’s dividend yield of 6.6% “likely puts in a floor.”

BofA was bearish on Amgen (NASDAQ:AMGN), Moderna (NASDAQ:MRNA) and Regeneron (NASDAQ:REGN), rating all three underperform. Price objectives were $256 for Amgen (AMGN), $41 for Moderna (MRNA) and $565 for Regeneron (REGN).

The bank noted that Amgen’s (AMGN) “obesity premium was coming out the stock—but we think there might be more room to go.” It also pointed out that the company was facing several patent expirations over the next decade.

For Moderna (MRNA), BofA noted “it was a tough time to be a vaccine company,” even if Robert F. Kennedy Jr. doesn’t become head of the Department of Health and Human Services. It added that it had “mixed views” on the company’s pipeline.

While BofA called Regeneron (REGN) a “high-quality company," it said it believes the company's Eylea franchise will erode quicker and deeper than expected.

data.title: Brookfield announces pricing of $700M subordinated notes due 2055
data.description:
• Brookfield Corporation (NYSE:BN) announced, on Tuesday, the pricing of a public offering of $700 million principal amount of subordinated notes due 2055.
  • The notes will bear interest at an annual rate of 6.300% until January 15, 2035 and thereafter will be reset every five years at an annual rate equal to the five-year U.S. treasury rate, plus a spread of 2.076%.
  • The notes will be issued by Brookfield Finance, an indirect 100% owned subsidiary of Brookfield, and will be fully and unconditionally guaranteed, on a subordinated basis, by Brookfield.
  • The net proceeds from the sale of the notes will be used for general corporate purposes. The offering is expected to close on December 17, 2024.
  • Brookfield Corporation (BN) shares were up 0.68% in post-market trading.

data.title: MAA ANNOUNCES CEO SUCCESSION PLAN
data.description:
• Mid-America Apartment Communities (NYSE:MAA), announced the appointment of A. Bradley Hill as its president and chief executive officer, effective April 1, 2025.
  • Hill will be succeeding H. Eric Bolton, Jr. who is retiring as CEO and will serve as executive chairman upon the effective date of the transition.
  • Bolton will continue to serve as chairman of the board of directors.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: GE Vernova maintains 2024 guidance, raises 2025 revenue view but still below consensus
data.description:
GE Vernova (NYSE:GEV) -3.2% post-market Tuesday after reaffirming guidance for FY 2024 revenues and free cash flow while raising guidance for 2025 revenues, free cash flow and adjusted EBITDA margin at the company's 2024 Investor Update.

The company sees FY 2024 revenues trending towards the higher end of guidance of $34B-$35B, compared to ~$35B analyst consensus estimate; adjusted EBITDA margin of 5.5%-6.0%, narrowed from its previous outlook for 5%-7%; and free cash flow trending towards higher end of $1.3B-$1.7B guidance.

For 2025, GE Vernova (GEV) forecasts revenues of $36B-$$37B, up from prior guidance of mid-single digit growth implying revenues of $35B-$37B but still below $37.3B analyst consensus; adjusted EBITDA margin of high-single digits, up from previous guidance for the low end of high single digits; and free cash flow of $2B-$2.5B, up from $1.2B-$1.8B previously.

By 2028, the company envisions high-single digits organic revenue growth, up from mid-single digits; adjusted EBITDA margin of 14%, up from 10%; and free cash flow of ~100% conversion, compared to 90%-110%.

GE Vernova (GEV) also declared a $0.25/share quarterly dividend and approved an initial $6B share repurchase authorization.

"We are executing our financial strategy, and we now expect to generate at least $14B in cumulative free cash flow by 2028," GE Vernova (GEV) CFO Ken Parks said.
